profile.name: Emily Wilson
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to drive the agenda and bring documents/screenshots, especially around investments and monthly cash flow.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High for investing basics and account types; comfortable discussing brokerage/cash flow, but may gloss over insurance/estate details unless prompted.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-oriented and willing to tolerate volatility if there’s a clear plan; tends to focus on long-term upside and can get impatient with overly conservative recommendations.
profile.communication_style: Fast-paced and organized, but uses hedging like "I think" and "roughly"; will say things like "I don’t remember the exact number" and then look it up; asks pointed questions and may jump ahead to outcomes.
emotional_tendencies: gets anxious when expenses feel tight month-to-month
relieved by clear checklists and next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Decides quickly once she sees a rationale and a timeline; prefers a recommended option with 1–2 alternatives rather than an open-ended menu.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously optimistic; will trust more after the advisor demonstrates accuracy and follows through on action items.
internal_biases: overconfidence in ability to "figure it out" without formal estate/protection planning
recency bias (reacts to recent market moves when discussing the brokerage account)
hidden_concerns: worries that high fixed housing costs limit flexibility and savings rate
concerned about whether they are "behind" on retirement given their ages and priorities

profile.name: James Ray Wilson
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; supportive but more reserved, often sanity-checks risk and asks clarifying questions, especially about retirement accounts and debt.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate; understands retirement plans and budgeting concepts, but prefers plain-English explanations for investment mechanics and tax nuances.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Generally comfortable with growth, but expresses it as "I’m okay with risk if we’re covered"; tends to push for safeguards (emergency cash, clear debt plan) before taking additional investment risk.
profile.communication_style: Calm and measured; uses fillers like "uh" and "so" and asks for definitions; may say "I’m not totally sure" or "I don’t remember exactly" and defer to Emily on account specifics; occasionally interrupts to confirm assumptions.
emotional_tendencies: stress spikes when discussing large fixed payments or deadlines
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; prefers to talk through pros/cons and sleep on big decisions, then align with Emily on a final call.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded at first; trust increases when the advisor explains tradeoffs clearly and doesn’t oversell.
internal_biases: loss aversion (focuses on what could go wrong before committing)
status quo bias (prefers small changes rather than big reallocations)
hidden_concerns: wants to ensure they have basic protections in place before focusing on estate planning goals
concerned about whether their retirement trajectory is realistic given competing priorities
